perturbation.original_text: 7.6 The Parties shall settle the payment every month, and the initial settlement period shall be the end of the third month, which means that the information service fee generated in the first month shall be paid to the account of the partner by the end of the third month. When requesting payment of relevant contract price from Party A, Party B has to: (1) provide official invoices that are in compliance with the requirements of State tax laws by the 25t h of the second month (Party B, instead of any of its affiliates, shall be the invoice issuer); (2) provide the payment request confirmed by the Parties; and (3) pay the performance bond as required in Article 6 hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: 7.6 The Parties shall settle the payment every month, and the initial settlement period shall be the end of the third month, which means that the information service fee generated in the first month shall be paid to the account of the partner by the end of the third month. When requesting payment of relevant contract price from Party A, Party B has to: (1) provide official invoices that are in compliance with the requirements of State tax laws by the 15th of the first month (Party B, instead of any of its affiliates, shall be the invoice issuer); (2) provide the payment request confirmed by the Parties; and (3) pay the performance bond as required in Article 6 hereof.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by altering the invoice submission deadline. Originally, invoices were due by the 25th of the second month. Now, they are due by the 15th of the first month. This contradicts the initial statement that payment will be settled at the end of the third month, creating uncertainty about the actual required timeline for invoice submission and payment processing. The contradiction affects the enforcement of timely payments and creates a potential dispute.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7.6 The Parties shall settle the payment every month, and the initial settlement period shall be the end of the third month, which means that the information service fee generated in the first month shall be paid to the account of the partner by the end of the third month. When requesting payment of relevant contract price from Party A, Party B has to: (1) provide official invoices that are in compliance with the requirements of State tax laws by the 15th of the first month (Party B, instead of any of its affiliates, shall be the invoice issuer); (2) provide the payment request confirmed by the Parties; and (3) pay the performance bond as required in Article 6 hereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the invoice submission deadline from the 25th of the second month to the 15th of the first month. This directly conflicts with the initial statement in both the original and 'contradicted' text, which states that payment will be settled at the end of the third month. This creates a clear and direct inconsistency regarding the required timeline for invoice submission, causing a strong in-text contradiction as defined by the prompt. The altered timeline for invoice submission is incompatible with the payment settlement timeline, creating ambiguity and potential disputes.

perturbation.original_text: 13.1 This Agreement shall come into force as of January 1, 2013 and remain valid for 1 year. As of the effective date hereof, the original cooperation of mobile game business executed between the Parties shall terminate automatically. Upon the expiry of this Agreement, this Agreement may be renewed automatically for one year (but can only be renewed once) if neither Party raises objection. Except otherwise agreed between the parties, the contents of this Agreement shall remain binding throughout the renewed period.
perturbation.changed_text: 13.1 This Agreement shall come into force as of January 1, 2013 and remain valid for 1 year. As of the effective date hereof, the original cooperation of mobile game business executed between the Parties shall terminate automatically. Upon the expiry of this Agreement, this Agreement may be renewed automatically for one year (and can be renewed multiple times) if neither Party raises objection. Except otherwise agreed between the parties, the contents of this Agreement shall remain binding throughout the renewed period.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces a contradiction regarding the renewal terms of the agreement. The original clause states the agreement can be renewed automatically for one year but can be renewed only once, while the modification allows for multiple renewals, creating uncertainty about the valid extent of the agreement and potential disputes. The contradiction affects the valid extent and enforcement of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 13.1 This Agreement shall come into force as of January 1, 2013 and remain valid for 1 year. As of the effective date hereof, the original cooperation of mobile game business executed between the Parties shall terminate automatically. Upon the expiry of this Agreement, this Agreement may be renewed automatically for one year (and can be renewed multiple times) if neither Party raises objection. Except otherwise agreed between the parties, the contents of this Agreement shall remain binding throughout the renewed period.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the renewal terms, allowing for multiple renewals instead of just one, directly contradicting the original text and causing uncertainty about the agreement's duration, which satisfies the definition of inconsistencies leading to in-text contradiction by creating conflicting obligations regarding the renewal terms.

perturbation.original_text: 6.1 Upon negotiations between the Parties, Party B will pay Party A RMB three thousand (3,000) as performance bond after this Agreement comes into force.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.1 Upon negotiations between the Parties, Party B will pay Party A RMB three thousand (3,000) as performance bond after this Agreement comes into force. This performance bond is fully refundable upon termination of this agreement regardless of any breaches made.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates an in-text contradiction regarding the refundability of the performance bond. The original clause does not specify the conditions for refunding the performance bond while the modification guarantees a full refund upon termination, irrespective of any breaches. This contradicts other sections that allow Party A to withhold the performance bond in case of breaches. The contradiction leads to uncertainty about whether Party A can withhold funds for breach of contract, thus creating ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.3 If any game, software, business, works, content or service provided by Party B has intellectual property defects or other right defects or other illegal issues, or Party A or any of its relevant bodies faces accusation, lawsuit, complaint, negative media report, administrative penalty, then Party A will temporarily withhold the settlement payment during the period when such dispute is pending for at least RMB50,000 per violation (business), and deduct from the performance bond already paid by Party B; at the same time, Party B shall pay an additional performance bond at an amount equivalent to the original one, within 10 working days upon the occurrence of the dispute. For the second time of the aforementioned violation, the amount of performance bond shall be additionally paid at RMB3,000 per time on the basis of the amount previous paid. For the third time of the aforementioned violation, Party A will withhold the full amount of the performance bond already paid by Party B, and terminate the full-range business cooperation for one year.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 6.1 guarantees a full refund of the performance bond upon termination, regardless of any breaches. However, section 6.3 allows Party A to withhold the performance bond if Party B violates intellectual property rights, faces accusations, lawsuits, etc. This creates a direct conflict: 6.1 states the bond is fully refundable regardless of breaches, while 6.3 states it can be withheld for breaches, creating a strong in-text contradiction.
